paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VII. Disjoint holes
summary: The overarching question of characterising which hereditary classes are polynomially χ-bounded remains open. After Briański, Davies and Walczak disproved Esperet's conjecture (that every χ-bounded hereditary class is poly-χ-bounded), the Pollyanna framework was introduced to organise which hereditary classes do satisfy a polynomial binding function, with specific forbidden-subgraph families now confirmed to be poly-χ-bounded. No complete characterisation is known as of 2026.

since_posted.title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VI. Adding a four-vertex path
since_posted.authors: Maria Chudnovsky, Alex Scott, Paul Seymour, Sophie Spirkl
since_posted.claim: Proves polynomial χ-boundedness for {H1, H2}-free graphs when every component of H1 is a good forest and H2 is a broom or P4, extending the catalogue of poly-χ-bounded hereditary classes.

since_posted.title: Pollyanna and Polynomially χ-Bounded Graph Classes
since_posted.claim: Proves three new strong Pollyanna results, including that for each t ≥ 2 the class of {diamond, hammer(t)⁺}-free graphs is t-strongly Pollyanna, advancing the programme of identifying which hereditary classes are poly-χ-bounded.

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed as a same-series paper by the same four authors proving polynomial χ-boundedness for specific H-free classes; directly contributes partial cases to the overarching question.
Not fetched within the 5-call budget; title indicates focus on suns in triangle-free graphs attributed to Trotignon; relevance to the poly-χ-bounded characterisation question is unclear.

Confirmed as a paper about treewidth of clock-free graphs (induced subgraph obstructions for large treewidth), not about poly-χ-bounded hereditary classes; the attributed contribution in the internal refs appears mismatched to this specific question.

search_queries: Briański Davies Walczak Esperet conjecture disproof poly-chi-bounded hereditary classes
poly-chi-bounded hereditary classes characterization chromatic number 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: Esperet's conjecture (every χ-bounded hereditary class is poly-χ-bounded) was disproved by Briański, Davies and Walczak (arXiv:2201.08814, published Combinatorica 2024); this is the direct motivation for the question as stated in the source paper. The Pollyanna framework — asking which hereditary classes C have the property that C ∩ F is poly-χ-bounded for every χ-bounded F — has been developed as a structured approach to the overarching question. A related but distinct direction (arXiv:2512.09176, Rahimi and Mojdeh 2025) studies an analogue using the domination parameter τ_d(G) rather than ω(G). Internal reference arXiv:2311.05719 appears to be a treewidth paper mismatched to this conjecture identifier.
